# NeoBase [actions] [cratev] [crated]

Minimalist [GeoBases](https://github.com/opentraveldata/geobases/)
implementation:

- no dependencies
- compatible with Python 3.9+, CPython and PyPy
- one data source:
  [opentraveldata](https://github.com/opentraveldata/opentraveldata)
- one Python module for easier distribution on clusters (like Hadoop)
- faster load time (5x)
- tested with pytest and tox

```python
>>> from neobase import NeoBase
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> b.get('ORY', 'city_code_list')
['PAR']
>>> b.get('ORY', 'city_name_list')
['Paris']
>>> b.get('ORY', 'country_code')
'FR'
>>> b.distance('ORY', 'CDG')
34.87...
>>> b.get_location('ORY')
LatLng(lat=48.72..., lng=2.35...)
```

## Installation

Use the Python package:

```bash
pip install neobase
```

## Docs

Check out [readthedocs](http://neobase.readthedocs.org/en/latest/) for the API.

You can customize the source data when initializing:

```python
with open("file.csv") as f:
    N = NeoBase(f)
```

Otherwise the loaded file will be the embedded one, unless the `OPTD_POR_FILE` environment variable is set. In that case, it will load from the path defined in that variable.

You can manually retrieve the latest data source yourself too, but you expose yourself to some breaking changes if they occur in the data.

```python
from io import StringIO
from urllib.request import urlopen

from neobase import NeoBase, OPTD_POR_URL

data = urlopen(OPTD_POR_URL).read().decode('utf8')
N = NeoBase(StringIO(data))
N.get("PAR")
```

The reference date of validity can be changed as well:

```python
N = NeoBase(date="2000-01-01")
N.get("AIY")  # was decommissioned in 2015
```

By default, the reference date will be set to today, unless the `OPTD_POR_DATE` environment variable is set. In that case, it will use that value.

You can customize the behavior regarding duplicates: points sharing the same IATA code, like NCE as airport and NCE as city. By default everything is kept, but you can set it so that only the first point with an IATA code is kept:

```python
N = NeoBase(duplicates=False)
len(N)  # about 10,000 "only"
```

Note that you can use the `OPTD_POR_DUPLICATES` environment variable to control this as well: set it to `0` to drop duplicates.

Finally, you can customize fields loaded by subclassing.

```python
class SubNeoBase(NeoBase):
    KEY = 0  # iata_code

    # Those loaded fields are the default ones
    FIELDS = (
        ("name", 6, None),
        ("lat", 8, None),
        ("lng", 9, None),
        ("page_rank", 12, lambda s: float(s) if s else None),
        ("country_code", 16, None),
        ("country_name", 18, None),
        ('continent_name', 19, None),
        ("timezone", 31, None),
        ("city_code_list", 36, lambda s: s.split(",")),
        ('city_name_list', 37, lambda s: s.split('=')),
        ('location_type', 41, None),
        ("currency", 46, None),
    )

N = SubNeoBase()
```

## Command-line interface

You can query the data using:

```bash
python -m neobase PAR NCE
```

## Tests

```bash
tox
```

classes.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.load
documentation:
Building a dictionary of geographical data from optd_por.

```pycon
>>> import os.path as op
>>> path = op.join(op.dirname(__file__), 'optd_por_public.csv')
>>> with open_(path) as f:
...     b = NeoBase.load(f, '2030-01-01', True)
>>> b['ORY']['city_code_list']
['PAR']
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.__iter__
documentation:
Returns iterator of all keys in the base.

```
returns

the iterator of all keys
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> sorted(b)
['AAA', 'AAA@1', 'AAB', ...
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.__contains__
documentation:
Test if a key is in the base.

```
param key

the key of to be tested

returns

a boolean
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> 'AN' in b
False
>>> 'AGN' in b
True
>>> 'agn' in b
True
>>> None in b
False
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.__bool__
documentation:
Testing structure emptiness.

```
returns

a boolean
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> if b:
...     print('not empty')
not empty
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.__len__
documentation:
Testing structure size.

```
returns

a integer
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> 18000 < len(b) < 20000
True
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.keys
documentation:
Returns iterator of all keys in the base.

```
returns

the iterator of all keys
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> sorted(b.keys())
['AAA', 'AAA@1', 'AAB', ...
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.set
documentation:
Set information.

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> b.get('ORY', 'name')
'Paris Orly Airport'
>>> b.set('ORY', name='test')
>>> b.get('ORY', 'name')
'test'
>>> b.set('Wow!', name='test')
>>> b.get('Wow!', 'name')
'test'
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.get
documentation:
Get data from structure.

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> b.get('OR', 'city_code_list', default=None)
>>> b.get('ORY', 'city_code_list')
['PAR']
>>> b.get('nce', 'city_code_list')
['NCE']
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.get_location
documentation:
Get None or the geocode.

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> b.get_location('ORY')
LatLng(lat=48.72..., lng=2.35...)
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.distance_between_locations
documentation:
Great circle distance

```
param l0

the LatLng tuple of the first location

param l1

the LatLng tuple of the second location

returns

the distance in kilometers
```

```pycon
>>> NeoBase.distance_between_locations((48.84, 2.367), (43.70, 7.26)) # Paris -> Nice
683.85...
```

Case of unknown location.

```pycon
>>> NeoBase.distance_between_locations(None, (43.70, 7.26)) # returns None
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.distance
documentation:
Compute distance between two elements.

This is just a wrapper between the original haversine
function, but it is probably the most used feature :)

```
param keys

the keys

returns

the distance (km)
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> b.distance('ORY', 'CDG')
34.87...
>>> b.distance('ORY', 'NCE', 'CDG')
1370.34...
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase._build_distances
documentation:
Compute the iterable of (dist, keys) of a reference
lat_lng and a list of keys. Keys which have not valid
geocodes will not appear in the results.

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> list(b._build_distances((0,0), ['ORY', 'CDG']))
[(5422.74..., 'ORY'), (5455.45..., 'CDG')]
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.find_near_location
documentation:
Returns a list of nearby keys from a location (given
latidude and longitude), and a radius for the search.
Note that the haversine function, which compute distance
at the surface of a sphere, here returns kilometers,
so the radius should be in kms.

```
param lat_lng

the lat_lng of the location

param radius

the radius of the search (kilometers)

param from_keys

if None, it takes all keys in consideration, else takes from_keys             iterable of keys to perform search.

returns

an iterable of (dist, key)
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> # Paris, airports <= 50km
>>> [b.get(k, 'iata_code') for d, k in sorted(b.find_near_location((48.84, 2.367), 5))]
['LYY', 'PAR', 'XGB', 'MPE', 'XHP', 'XPG', 'XEX']
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.find_near
documentation:
Same as find_near_location, except the location is given
not by a lat/lng, but with its key, like ORY or SFO.
We just look up in the base to retrieve lat/lng, and
call find_near_location.

```
param key

the key of the location

param radius

the radius of the search (kilometers)

param from_keys

if None, it takes all keys in consideration, else takes from_keys             iterable of keys to perform search.

returns

an iterable of (dist, key)
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> sorted(b.find_near('ORY', 10)) # Orly, por <= 10km
[(0.0, 'ORY'), (6.94..., 'XJY'), (9.96..., 'QFC')]
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.find_closest_from_location
documentation:
Concept close to find_near_location, but here we do not
look for the keys radius-close to a location,
we look for the closest key from this location, given by
latitude/longitude.

```
param lat_lng

the lat_lng of the location

param N

the N closest results wanted

param from_keys

if None, it takes all keys in consideration, else takes from_keys             iterable of keys to perform find_closest_from_location. This is useful to combine             searches

returns

an iterable of (dist, key)
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> list(b.find_closest_from_location((43.70, 7.26))) # Nice
[(0.60..., 'NCE@1')]
>>> list(b.find_closest_from_location((43.70, 7.26), N=3)) # Nice
[(0.60..., 'NCE@1'), (5.82..., 'NCE'), (5.89..., 'XBM')]
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.find_closest_from
documentation:
Same as find_closest_from_location, except the location is given
not by a lat/lng, but with its key, like ORY or SFO.
We just look up in the base to retrieve lat/lng, and
call find_closest_from_location.

```
param key

the key of the location

param N

the N closest results wanted

param from_keys

if None, it takes all keys in consideration, else takes from_keys             iterable of keys to perform find_closest_from_location. This is useful to combine             searches

returns

an iterable of (dist, key)
```

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> list(b.find_closest_from('NCE'))
[(0.0, 'NCE')]
>>> list(b.find_closest_from('NCE', N=3))
[(0.0, 'NCE'), (5.07..., 'XCG@1'), (5.45..., 'XCG')]
```

methods.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.find_with
documentation:
Get iterator of all keys with particular field.

For example, if you want to know all airports in Paris.

```
param conditions

a list of (field, value) conditions

param reverse

we look keys where the field is not the particular value

returns

an iterator of matching keys
```

Testing several conditions.

```pycon
>>> b = NeoBase()
>>> c0 = [('city_code_list', ['PAR'])]
>>> c1 = [('location_type', ['H'])]
>>> len(list(b.find_with(c0)))
18
>>> len(list(b.find_with(c0 + c1)))
2
```

class_variables.name: neobase.neobase.NeoBase.KEY
documentation: iata_code
Main structure, a wrapper around a dict, with dict-like behavior.
